description.task: Implement an AVLChecker class that checks whether a binary tree satisfies the AVL property (|height(left) - height(right)| <= 1 for every node). Also compute the balance factor of each node.
instructions: isAVL: if root==null return true; if Math.abs(balanceFactor(root))>1 return false; return isAVL(root.left)&&isAVL(root.right)
balanceFactor and height are already implemented
Balanced tree {10,5{3,7},15}: all BFs are -1,0,1 → isAVL=true
Unbalanced chain 10→5→3→1: height difference at 10 is 3 vs 0 → isAVL=false
printBalanceFactors prints inorder (left sorted) showing each node's balance factor
description.hint: isAVL: if(root==null)return true; if(Math.abs(balanceFactor(root))>1)return false; return isAVL(root.left)&&isAVL(root.right);
description.expectedOutput:
Balanced AVL: true
Node 3: BF=0
Node 5: BF=0
Node 7: BF=0
Node 10: BF=1
Node 15: BF=0
Unbalanced: false

description.task: Implement an AVLRotations class with right rotation, left rotation, and the composite left-right and right-left rotations used in AVL tree balancing. Demonstrate each rotation on a small subtree.
instructions: rotateLeft(x): Node y=x.right; Node T2=y.left; y.left=x; x.right=T2; updateHeight(x); updateHeight(y); return y;
rotateRight is already implemented — mirror it for rotateLeft
After right rotating 30→20→10: new root is 20 with left=10, right=30
rotateLR and rotateRL use the two single rotations
description.hint: rotateLeft: Node y=x.right; Node T2=y.left; y.left=x; x.right=T2; updateHeight(x); updateHeight(y); return y;
description.expectedOutput:
Before right rotation:
30(h=3)
L:20(h=2)
L:10(h=1)
After right rotation:
20(h=2)
L:10(h=1)
R:30(h=1)

description.task: Implement a SimpleAVL class that inserts integers and automatically rebalances using AVL rotations. Implement insert with balance factor checks and appropriate single/double rotations.
instructions: LR case: if(balance>1&&val>node.left.val){ node.left=rotateLeft(node.left); return rotateRight(node); }
RL case: if(balance<-1&&val<node.right.val){ node.right=rotateRight(node.right); return rotateLeft(node); }
After inserting [30,20,10,25,40]: AVL tree is balanced, inorder = 10 20 25 30 40, root=20 or 25 depending on rotations
description.hint: Add the 4 if-blocks before 'return node': LL→return rotateRight; RR→return rotateLeft; LR→left=rotateLeft(left); return rotateRight; RL→right=rotateRight(right); return rotateLeft.
